keynotes.text: J. Meredith (H. W., xxxiii. 127) has recorded the effects observed on a science student from grinding up this salt and inhaling the fumes. Eye-soreness, coryza, sore throat and chest with difficult breathing, were the main symptoms. They lasted fourteen days from the exposure; and were cured by Bell. 3x internally and a one per cent. Iodine lotion locally as a compress at night.

generalities.text: In morning breathing difficult; lungs wheezy; chest and throat sore.

eyes.text: Great soreness of mucous membranes of eyes and nose, with copious intermittent runnings; lasts all night. Shades eyes from light reflected from tablecloth at dinner. Whites of eyes congested.

nose.text: Irritation and copious intermittent running. The intolerable smell of the fumes cannot be forgotten at night, and sleep is impossible.

sleep.text: Symptoms kept him awake all night. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.
